description: Reduce VIX_BULL_HARD_CAP from 22.0 to 18.0 to block BULL (CALL) entries when VIX > 18.0. Hypothesis: VIX 18-22 bull entries are net losers.

sub_window_analysis.summary: UNSTABLE — W1 HURT. Improvement concentrated in high-VIX 2026 periods (W3+W4). Normal 2025 market (W1) suffers.

oos_detail.anomaly_note: Removed OOS trade is a PUT (bear entry, side=P). Expected: vix_bull_max only blocks CALL (bull) entries. Why a bear entry is removed when reducing the bull cap from 22->18 requires investigation in filters.py VIX_BULL_HARD_CAP usage context.

ratification_gates.sub_window_note: W1 Jan-Jun 2025 HURT (-$372). 2 IS bull entries in VIX 18-22 were profitable in calm 2025 market. Regime-conditional improvement only.

mechanism: Blocks BULL (CALL) entries when VIX > 18.0. In high-VIX post-shock environments (2026), bull entries at VIX 18-22 are net losers. In calm markets (2025, VIX 15-17), VIX momentarily crosses 18 on bearish days when BULL reversals ARE profitable. Filter is regime-conditional.
root_cause: The IS improvement (+$1,253) and OOS improvement (+$219) are driven by 2026 high-VIX conditions (W3+W4). In 2025 (W1), the 2 removed IS bull entries were profitable. The filter works when VIX-base is high (18+ is the normal level) but hurts when VIX is low (18 is a temporary spike on an otherwise calm day).
next_steps: Investigate why PUT entry on 2026-05-13 VIX=18.3 is removed by bull cap change (grep VIX_BULL_HARD_CAP in filters.py)
Consider regime-specific deployment: enable vix_bull_max=18.0 only after VIX has been > 20 for 5+ days (prevents W1 HURT). Kitchen task candidate.
